---
title: "Development and evaluation of Reinforcement Learning strategies for landing a simulated 1:10th scale SpaceX Falcon 9 using the `PyFlyt Rocket-Landing-v0` environment."
institute: "Coventry University"
author: "Leandro Landgraf"
date: "August 2023"
header-includes: 
  - \usepackage{amsmath}
output: 
  beamer_presentation:
    number_sections: true
    slide_level: 2
    theme: "Boadilla"
    colortheme: "whale"
    fonttheme: "professionalfonts"
bibliography: references.bib
csl: american-psychological-association-7th-edition.csl
nocite: '@*'
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
```

## Overview
\tableofcontents[hideallsubsections]

# Introduction
## Problem Statement
- Development and evaluation of RL methods and algorithms for landing a simulated 1:10th scale SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, falling at terminal velocity on a landing pad, with only 1% of fuel remaining.

- The simulated rocket is a component of the `PyFlyt Rocket-Landing-v0` environment.

## Research Question(s)
  1. How can reward functions for reinforcement learning systems be defined?
  2. Is it possible to successfully solve the `PyFlyt/Rocket-Landing-v0` environment? 
  3. Can a reward function developed using a given algorithm be solved by another one, under the same circumstances (i.e., do reward functions overfit to a particular algorithm)?

## Aims and Objectives
- Investigate proposed solutions to the problem (traditional and machine-learning based).
- Describe the type of simulated environments used in other applications and the `PyFlyt/Rocket-Landing-v0` environment.
- Identify and select reinforcement learning algorithms and methods suitable for solving `Rocket-Landing-v0`
- Define and implement the most adequate reward functions to allow `Rocket-Landing-v0` to be solvable.
- Implement the selected reinforcement learning methods.
- Demonstrate the feasibility of using reinforcement learning methods to accomplish the control and landing of reusable rockets.
- Evaluate the implemented algorithms and reward functions.
- Compare and analyse the results obtained.


# Background Research
## Reusable Launch Vehicles
- Challenges:
  - Extreme environmental factors and unknowns
  - Reduced margin for error
  - Demand for precise navigation and guidance systems
  - Error-free propulsion and landing control
  
\vspace{2mm} 
- Advantages:
  - Potential to dramatically reduce the cost of space launches
  - Increased accessibility and frequency of launches
  - Reduction of the environmental footprint
  - Advancements in technology and engineering
  - Increased space tourism and commercialization

## Solutions and alternatives
- Traditional and MPC, convex optimization:
  - Simplified models [@zaragoza2020guidance]
  - Complex models [@simplicio2019reusable; @simplicio2019guidance; @gettatelli2023convex; @lee2022multi]

\vspace{2mm} 
- RL-based, 2D very simplified models, mixed results:
  - Comparison with traditional control [@ferrante2017robust]
  - Simple 1D and 2D with curriculum learning and PPO [@kaiser2021optimal]
  - Curriculum RL with TD3 on a 3DoF plane rigid body model [@yuan2021leveraging]
  - GA search of reward function [@larasati2022genetic]
  - PPO and DDPG [@souza2022thrust]
  - GA and comparison with other methods [@nugroho2023optimization]
- RL-based, 3D models:
  - Combination of two techniques: curriculum and imitation learning [@leguet2021landing]

# Methods
## Reinforcement Learning
- MDP of the form $M = \langle {S}, {A}, {T}, \gamma, R \rangle$
  - $S$ is the state space, $A = \{a_{1}, ..., a_{k} \}$ with $k\geq2$ is the action space, $T(s,a,s') = Pr(s'|s,a)$ are the next stage transition probabilities, $\gamma \in (0,1]$ is the discount factor, and $R(s,a,s')$ the reward function defining $r$ received when taking action $a$ in state $s$ resulting in transitioning to state $s'$
  
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.45\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/agent-environment.jpg}
\end{figure}
- Solution to $M$: to learn an optimum  policy $\pi^{*}: S \to A$ that maximizes the expected total sum of discounted rewards $\sum_{t=0}^{\infty} \gamma^{t}r_{t}$

## Reinforcement Learning Methods
### PPO
- Leverages vectorized environments to improve learning speed
- On-policy algorithm
- Can be used for either discrete or continuous action spaces

\vspace{5mm}
\tiny
https://spinningup.openai.com/en/latest/algorithms/ppo.html

https://docs.cleanrl.dev/rl-algorithms/ppo/

### SAC
- Off-policy algorithm
- Suitable for continuous action spaces
- Characterized by use of *entropy regularization* 

\vspace{5mm}
\tiny
https://spinningup.openai.com/en/latest/algorithms/sac.html

https://docs.cleanrl.dev/rl-algorithms/sac/

## PyFlyt Rocket-Landing-v0
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.45\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/env_gif1.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.45\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/env_gif2.png}
\end{figure}

- Action space: $\mathbf{a} = [\nu_{x}, \nu_{y}, \nu_{yaw}, \iota, \beta, \xi_{x}, \xi_{y}]$
- Observation space: $\mathbf{s} = [\mathbf{\dot\theta_{B}}, \mathbf{\theta_{G}}, \mathbf{\dot{p}}_{B}, \mathbf{p}_{G}, \mathbf{a}_{t-1}, \mathbf{s}_{aux}, \mathbb{I}_{pad}, \mathbf{d}_{G}]$

## Original Landing Criteria
\footnotesize
$\mathbb{I}_{pad} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if landing pad contact} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$

$\mathbb{I}_{collision} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } [(\dot\theta_{t-1} > 0.35 rad/s) \lor (v_{t-1} > 1.0 m/s)] \land \mathbb{I}_{pad} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$

$\mathbb{I}_{landed} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } [(\dot\theta_{t-1} < 0.02 rad/s) \land (v_{t-1}<0.02 m/s) \land (\theta_t<0.1 rad)] \land \mathbb{I}_{pad} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$

- Problems:
  - There is a "deadband" between $\mathbb{I}_{collision}$ and $\mathbb{I}_{landed}$
  - The maximum allowed linear velocity is too low 
  - No overturn stability criteria considered from "crashing" (estimated to be $\approx 0.4 rad$)
  - Criteria for $\mathbb{I}_{landed}$ are possibly too restrictive

\vspace{5mm}
\color{white}
\tiny
https://cosmosmagazine.com/space/launch-land-repeat-reusable-rockets-explained/

https://www.teslarati.com/how-spacex-achieves-falcon-9-rocket-landing-accuracy/

https://space.stackexchange.com/questions/8771/how-stable-would-a-falcon-9-first-stage-be-after-it-has-landed-on-a-drone-ship


## Proposed Landing Criteria
\footnotesize
$\mathbb{I}_{pad} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if landing pad contact} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$

$\mathbb{I}_{collision} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } [(\dot\theta_{t-1} \geq 0.35 rad/s) \lor (v_{t-1} \geq 5.56 m/s) \lor (\theta_{t} \geq 0.2 rad)] \land \mathbb{I}_{pad} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$

$\mathbb{I}_{landed} = \begin{cases} 1 & \text{if } [(\dot\theta_{t-1} < 0.35 rad/s) \land (v_{t-1}<5.56 m/s) \land (\theta_t<0.2 rad)] \land \mathbb{I}_{pad} \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$

- Changes:
  - Removes the "deadband" between $\mathbb{I}_{collision}$ and $\mathbb{I}_{landed}$
  - Maximum allowed linear velocity of 20 km/h 
  - Overturn stability criteria of $0.2 rad$ included for "fatal collision" condition
  - Less restrictive criteria for $\mathbb{I}_{landed}$


## Reward Function Design for RL
- Reward shaping: to include additional rewards to the learning agent
  - Provides more frequents feedback to agent
  - Helps guide the learning process
  - Turns sparse reward to dense reward
  - Can improve learning speed

\vspace{2mm}
- Provide function based on state, using ***potential based*** shaping function [@ng1999policy]:
  - $F(s, a, s') = \gamma \Phi(s') - \Phi(s)$
  - Results in: $M' = \langle S, A, T, \gamma, R' \rangle$ with $R' = R + F$

## $R^{0} \;\;\; {\alpha_{0}} = 0$
- Original [@tai2023pyflyt]

$R^{0} = r_{d} + r_{v} + \mathbb{I}_{pad}r_{pad} - 100\mathbb{I}_{ground}$ 

\footnotesize  
$r_{d} = -0.2 \cdot d_{pad} \;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\; r_{v} = -d^{-2}_{pad} \cdot v \;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\; r_{pad} = \begin{cases} -20 & \text{if fatal,} \\ 100 & \text{if safe} \end{cases}$

\normalsize
\vspace{2mm}
- Currently:

$R^{0}_{sparse} = {\alpha_{7_{0}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{8_{0}}} \times \neg\mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{9_{0}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{collision} + {\alpha_{10_{0}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{landed}$

$R^{0}_{dense} = - {\alpha^0_{1}} + (\frac{{\alpha^0_{2}}}{d_{pad}+0.1}) + ({\alpha^0_{3}} \times \Delta d_{pad}) - ({\alpha^0_{4}} \times |\dot\theta_{z}|) - ({\alpha^0_{5}} \times \theta) + R^{0}_{sparse}$ 

\footnotesize
$\mathbf{\alpha^0_{1}} = \{ 1, 3, 100, 1, 3, 1, 0, 20, 0, 0, 500 \}$

$\mathbf{\alpha^0_{2}} = \{ 0, 5, 1, 100, 2, 5, 0, 50, 0, -50, 1000 \}$

## $\Phi_{0} \;\;\; {\alpha_{0}} = 1$
$R = {\alpha_{7_{0}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{8_{0}}} \times \neg\mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{9_{0}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{collision} + {\alpha_{10_{0}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{landed}$

$\Phi_0 = -\lVert \mathbf{s} \rVert \;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\; \mathbf{s} \in \{\mathbf{d_{pad}}, \mathbf{\dot\theta}, \mathbf{\theta}, \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{f_{rem}} \}$

$F(s, a, s')_0 = {\alpha_{i_{0}}} \times \left( -\gamma \times \lVert \mathbf{s'} \rVert + \lVert \mathbf{s} \rVert \right) \;\;\;\;\;\;\;\; 2 \leq i \leq 6$
\begin{equation}
\begin{split}
R'_0 = & - {\alpha_{1_{0}}} + ({\alpha_{2_{0}}} \times (-\gamma \times \lVert {d_{pad}'} \rVert + \lVert {d_{pad}} \rVert)) + \\
& ({\alpha_{3_{0}}} \times (-\gamma \times \lVert {\dot\theta'} \rVert + \lVert {\dot\theta} \rVert)) + \\
& ({\alpha_{4_{0}}} \times (-\gamma \times \lVert {\theta'} \rVert + \lVert {\theta} \rVert)) + \\
& ({\alpha_{5_{0}}} \times (-\gamma \times \lVert {v'} \rVert + \lVert {v} \rVert)) + \\
& ({\alpha_{6_{0}}} \times (-\gamma \times \lVert {f_{rem}'} \rVert + \lVert {f_{rem}} \rVert)) + R
\end{split}
\end{equation}
\footnotesize
$\mathbf{\alpha_{1_{0}}} = \{ 1, 3, 100, 1, 3, 1, 0, 20, 0, 0, 500 \}$

$\mathbf{\alpha_{2_{0}}} = \{ 1, 5, 5, 2, 5, 3, 0, 50, 0, -50, 1000 \}$

## $\Phi_{1} \;\;\; {\alpha_{0}} = 2$
$R = {\alpha_{7_{1}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{8_{1}}} \times \neg\mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{9_{1}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{collision} + {\alpha_{10_{1}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{landed}$

$\Phi_1 = \left( \frac{1}{\lVert \mathbf{s} \rVert} \right) \;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\; \mathbf{s} \in \{\mathbf{d_{pad}}, \mathbf{\dot\theta}, \mathbf{\theta}, \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{f_{rem}} \}$

$F(s, a, s')_1 = {\alpha_{i_{1}}} \times \left( \frac{\gamma}{\lVert \mathbf{s'} \rVert} - \frac{1}{\lVert \mathbf{s} \rVert} \right) \;\;\;\;\;\;\;\; 2 \leq i \leq 6$
\begin{equation}
\begin{split}
R'_1 = & - {\alpha_{1_{1}}} + {\alpha_{2_{1}}} \times \left( \frac{\gamma}{\lVert \mathbf{d_{pad}'} \rVert} - \frac{1}{\lVert \mathbf{d_{pad}} \rVert} \right) + \\
& {\alpha_{3_{1}}} \times \left( \frac{\gamma}{\lVert \mathbf{\dot\theta'} \rVert} - \frac{1}{\lVert \mathbf{\dot\theta} \rVert} \right) + {\alpha_{4_{1}}} \times \left( \frac{\gamma}{\lVert \mathbf{\theta'} \rVert} - \frac{1}{\lVert \mathbf{\theta} \rVert} \right) + \\ 
& {\alpha_{5_{1}}} \times \left( \frac{\gamma}{\lVert \mathbf{v'} \rVert} - \frac{1}{\lVert \mathbf{v} \rVert} \right) + {\alpha_{6_{1}}} \times \left( \frac{\gamma}{\lVert \mathbf{f_{rem}'} \rVert} - \frac{1}{\lVert \mathbf{f_{rem}} \rVert} \right) + R
\end{split}
\end{equation}
\footnotesize
$\mathbf{\alpha_{1_{1}}} = \{ 2, 3, 1000, 300, 10, 500, 0, 20, 0, 0, 500 \}$

$\mathbf{\alpha_{2_{1}}} = \{ 2, 5, 500, 150, 5, 250, 0, 50, 0, -50, 1000 \}$

## $\Phi_{2} \;\;\; {\alpha_{0}} = 3$
$R = {\alpha_{7_{2}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{8_{2}}} \times \neg\mathbb{I}_{pad} + {\alpha_{9_{2}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{collision} + {\alpha_{10_{2}}} \times \mathbb{I}_{landed}$

$\Phi_2 = \left( - \log _{10} (\mathbf{s} + 1) \right) \;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\;\; \mathbf{s} \in \{\mathbf{d_{pad}}, \mathbf{\dot\theta}, \mathbf{\theta}, \mathbf{v}, \mathbf{f_{rem}} \}$

$F(s, a, s')_2 = {\alpha_{i_{2}}} \times \left( -\gamma \times \log _{10} (s' + 1) + \log _{10} (s + 1) \right)$
\begin{equation}
\begin{split}
R'_2 = & - {\alpha_{1_{2}}} + {\alpha_{2_{2}}} \times \left( -\gamma \times \log _{10} (d_{pad}' + 1) + \log _{10} (d_{pad} + 1) \right) + \\
& {\alpha_{3_{2}}} \times \left( -\gamma \times \log _{10} (\dot\theta' + 1) + \log _{10} (\dot\theta + 1) \right) + \\
& {\alpha_{4_{2}}} \times \left( -\gamma \times \log _{10} (\theta' + 1) + \log _{10} (\theta + 1) \right) + \\
& {\alpha_{5_{2}}} \times \left( -\gamma \times \log _{10} (v' + 1) + \log _{10} (v + 1) \right) + \\
& {\alpha_{6_{2}}} \times \left( -\gamma \times \log _{10} (f_{rem}' + 1) + \log _{10} (f_{rem} + 1) \right) + R
\end{split}
\end{equation}
\footnotesize
$\mathbf{\alpha_{1_{2}}} = \{ 3, 3, 2, 2, 1, 5, 0, 20, 0, 0, 500 \}$

$\mathbf{\alpha_{2_{2}}} = \{ 3, 5, 3, 3, 2, 3, 0, 50, 0, -50, 1000 \}$

## Curriculum Learning
- Based on ideas from @yuan2021leveraging and @kaiser2021optimal
  - Two-stage learning:
    - First stage: lean to stabilize attitude ($\theta$), minimize velocity ($v$) and spin $\dot\theta$; no fuel limitation at this stage
    - Second-stage: include fuel limitation (progressively, down to 1%) and aim to landing at the pad (minimize position error)
  - Use of a multiplicative "curriculum learning factor" $\alpha_{cl} \in [1,10]$ that broadens the success criteria and is controlled by $\mathbb{I}_{landed}$:
$\alpha_{cl} = \begin{cases} -1 & \text{if } \mathbb{I}_{landed}  \\ +1 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$

- Pending implementation.

# Experimentation and Results
## Testing
- For each proposal, 10 random seeds, $1 \times 10^6$ episodes
  - Due to time constraints, for proposals only $350k$ steps, logging at every $5k$
- Showing only first $800k$ episodes due to inconsistencies when running tests
- Graphics showing running average with smoothing factor 10 and standard deviation as shadowed area

## Results - Baseline 1/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/best_eval_base.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/reward_base.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/fitness_base.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/frem_base.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - Baseline 2/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/dpad_base.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/lin_vel_base.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_pos_base.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_vel_base.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $R^0$ 1/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/best_eval_r0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/reward_r0.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/fitness_r0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/frem_r0.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $R^0$ 2/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/dpad_r0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/lin_vel_r0.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_pos_r0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_vel_r0.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $\Phi_{0}$ 1/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/best_eval_phi0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/reward_phi0.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/fitness_phi0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/frem_phi0.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $\Phi_{0}$ 2/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/dpad_phi0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/lin_vel_phi0.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_pos_phi0.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_vel_phi0.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $\Phi_{1}$ 1/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/best_eval_phi1.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/reward_phi1.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/fitness_phi1.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/frem_phi1.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $\Phi_{1}$ 2/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/dpad_phi1.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/lin_vel_phi1.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_pos_phi1.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_vel_phi1.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $\Phi_{2}$ 1/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/best_eval_phi2.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/reward_phi2.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/fitness_phi2.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/frem_phi2.png}
\end{figure}

## Results - $\Phi_{2}$ 2/2
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/dpad_phi2.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/lin_vel_phi2.png}
\end{figure}
\vspace{5mm}
\begin{figure}
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_pos_phi2.png}
   \hfill
   \includegraphics[width=0.475\textwidth]{../dissertation/images/ang_vel_phi2.png}
\end{figure}


# Conclusion and Discussions
## Challenges and Limitations
- Complex problem and environment
  - Most RL-based and other similar academic research use simplified models
- Learning/training process very slow (around 15 hours for each run)
- Many different runs required for reliable evaluations
  - Originally planned for 50, but due to time constraints reduced to only 10 ($\approx$ 1 week)

## Conclusion and next steps
- No proposal successfully solved the environment
- For each proposal, vectors $\mathbf{\alpha_i}$ have to be better calibrated
- PBRS may be a good solution for solvable environments when the real value function is known
- A lot of domain knowledge is required to define shaping functions

- Next steps:
  - Change $\mathbf{\alpha_i}$ to improve results
    - Possibly using automated searching algorithms (BO, GA)
  - Mix the different $\Phi_i$ based on the best outcomes for the relevant observation space variables
  - Solve the environment using SAC
  - Keeping the same reward function, apply a different RL algorithm (PPO)


# References {.allowframebreaks}
<div id="refs"></div>
